channel.title: Docs on Jin
channel.description: Recent content in Docs on Jin

item.title: Rerank EndeavourOS mirrors
item.description:
To rerank Arch mirrors, follow this guide.

Endeavour OS has its own distro-unique packages, which may be slightly modified versions of Arch packages.

  1. Update the Endeavour OS mirrorlist with:

    $ eos-rankmirrors

Note
The eos-rankmirrors process only shows terminal output (no GUI tool).

  1. The terminal may display errors/warnings as the system tests various mirrors for connectivity and speed. It may take a few minutes to find the requisite amount of mirrors.

item.title: Rerank Arch mirrors
item.description:
To rerank EndeavourOS mirrors, follow this guide.

  • Overview
  • Common issues: outdated mirrors

Mirrors are servers located around the world that store copies of software packages.

When upgrading packages, your EndeavourOS system utilizes multiple mirrors, which are noted in a mirrorlist.

A well-maintained mirrorlist ensures the pacman package manager can access the most up-to-date package files when performing system updates. Outdated mirrors can prevent pacman from updating applications, including core packages.

Important!
It is recommended to re-rank mirrors at least every 1-2 months.

item.title: Change default kernel
item.description:
Note: This guide was originally published on GitHub and is also available in Confluence.

Contents

  1. Find kernel id with bootctl
  2. Edit boot loader config
  3. Verify and reboot
  4. Additional information

Overview

Your system can have multiple kernels (or installations) of the same Linux distro. This is typically from having different ISO images, such as EOS Mercury, Titan, and Ganymede. The newest kernel is usually booted by default, but can be adjusted to user preference.

item.title: Downgrade to specific date
item.description:
Note: This guide was originally published on GitHub and is also available in Confluence.

Downgrade packages with eos-shifttime

The eos-shifttime application can revert system packages to their state at a specified date. This tool is useful for when recent updates cause issues with system or program functionalities.

  1. Open a terminal window and run eos-shifttime with root permissions.

  2. Select the date to revert to on the GUI window, then click Revert.

item.title: Update with pacman
item.description:
Arch systems should be updated daily to weekly. There are 3 commands used to Endeavour OS: pacman -Syu, yay, and eos-update.

  • pacman -Syu
  • yay
  • eos-update

pacman -Syu

To perform a full system update and refresh, run:

$ sudo pacman -Syu

pacman is the package manager used to install and update programs in Arch Linux.

Some commands require appending sudo to them - this is because they require root permissions. These elevated permissions allows the superuser to do system-related tasks, such as updating the computer.

item.title: EndeavourOS Newbie FAQs
item.description:
Why am I getting errors when I try to update?

Some update errors are caused by issues with mirrors and mirrorlist configurations. These issues can be fixed by re-ranking mirrors.

Important!
Outdated package databases will cause errors when pacman tries to retrieve the latest package files. This can be fixed through regular mirror maintenance.

See: [Update mirrors guide][Update-mirrors]

Can I undo or revert updates?

The eos-shifttime tool allows users to revert system packages to versions of specified dates.
